observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive physical count as early as possible to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same on or before January 31 of the ensuing year;
Direct the General Services Office to transfer all identified unserviceable properties in the IIRUP Form for proper disposal and basis to derecognize from the books of accounts; and
Direct all concerned officials to undertake procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the availment of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Take up adjusting entries to correct the misstated amount based on the Status of Work Accomplished (SWA) as of December 31, 2024, and to submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher to the COA Office;
Ensure that for projects with no received billing from contractors, the amount to be booked as AP must be based on the SWA as of year-end, while for projects that have not yet started but whose contract costs have been obligated, no part of their cost should be booked as AP; and
Record payments in the ensuing year and thereafter upon receipt of the Progress Billing as follows:
CIP-Appropriate Asset Account	xxx
Accounts Payable				xxx
Accounts Payable			xxx
Cash						xxx
The Municipal Engineer to:
Submit the SWA at year-end to the Accounting Unit for projects with no progress billings from contractors as basis for the recording of AP.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to:
Conduct comprehensive physical count as early as possible to give enough time to prepare the RPCI; and
Henceforth, submit the 1st and 2nd semester RPCI on or before July 31 and January 31, respectively.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain and submit subsidiary ledgers to support figures presented in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Engineering Office agreed to coordinate in the determination of charging of repairs and maintenance so as to avoid misclassifications.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Demand from the concerned Accountable Officers the immediate liquidation of cash advances outstanding as of year-end; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional cash advance(s) be issued unless the previous cash advance given to them is first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Issue a memorandum reminding all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports with complete supporting documents within 60 days after return to the Philippines in the case of foreign travel or within 30 days after return to his permanent official station in the case of local travel; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Demand immediate liquidation of cash advances outstanding as of year-end; and
Ensure that no additional cash advance(s) to any officials or employees be issued unless the previous cash advance given to them is first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Exert more effort in the collection of delinquent real property taxes;
Post a list notice of the delinquency at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the local government unit; and
Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor’s Office to determine how to expedite the updating of records of the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
The LDRRMC prepare and submit a revised LDRRMFIP as basis for any revision to the PPAs to be charged to the LDRRMF and is duly supported by an approved revised AIP;
The LDRRMC make sure that revised LDRRMFIP is consistent with any supplemental budget and with all other related reports such as the Monthly Utilization Report and SAAOB; and
The LDRRMC exert effort in implementing programs and activities identified in the LDRRMFIP provided that the PPAs identified are indeed chargeable to the LDRRMF.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to institute proper measures in ensuring that the program, project and activities in the GPB are adequately implemented and continue to monitor compliance thereof pursuant to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM NEDA-JMC No. 2013-01, in order to attain the GAD objectives.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed ensure the submission of the annual GPB and the corresponding AR to the Audit Team within the prescribed period. Further, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the different departments to submit their accomplishment reports on time to the GFPS to aid in the efficient consolidation of the AR of the preceding year.

We recommended that the Disbursing Officer promptly return any excess or unutilized cash advances upon liquidation and ensure the proper and timely recording of cash advances in the cashbook to enhance transparency and adherence with the generally accepted accounting principle.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly enforce immediate settlement of the above settlements by complying with all the requirements indicated in the respective notices of suspension with the assistance of the Municipal Legal Officer, otherwise unsettled transactions will be disallowed.
We recommended and management agreed to henceforth make sure that AAPSI for all future AARs be submitted completely filled out and within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the management agreed to pursue the same commitment every year and maintain an efficient planning and procurement process, along with strict monitoring and supervision of the engineering aspects of construction projects, while also working on the partially or unimplemented PPAs; and
Ensure proper maintenance and utilize the heavy equipment for development projects of the LGU.

To submit list of Detailed Computations required to support the Program of Works; and

Copy of confirmation/ formal advice from the Investment Coordination Committee (ICC) of the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Board on the reasonable rate of return on investments and operating and maintenance cost on project proposal of ECOS Sanitary Landfill and Waste Management Corporation as required under Section 2.6.b of the IRR of R.A. 7718.

Comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said Circular for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission On Audit.
The LDRRMC should identify in its LDRRMFIP only PPAs that are clear and implementable.

The Municipal Accountant make sure that all travel claims are duly supported by complete supporting documents as provided by pertinent laws and regulations;

The Municipal Mayor should only issue the certification of absolute necessity when the assigned task of the officials and employees necessitates the availment of actual accommodation expenses that exceed the accommodation component of the DTE;
Vacant regular positions should be filled up first and redundant or unnecessary job orders such as those that duplicate the work of regular employees should be limited.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to include in the certification of absolute necessity explanation/justification as to why claims exceeding the accommodation component are necessary. Claims with justifications found not in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-003 or the Updated Guidelines for the Prevention and Disallowance of Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant and Unconscionable Expenditures will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of hiring vehicles when circumstance do not warrant the same, and that the Municipal Accountant make sure that travel claims for “Van” are duly supported by evidence that such is a customary mode of transport in the area.

Hence, to preclude double payment, it is recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require PNP personnel to attach certifications from the PNP Accounting Office that the said travel claim has not been charged to their office budget.

observations.recommendation:
To submit list of Detailed Computaitons required to support the Program of Works; and

Copy of confirmation/formal advice from the Investment Coordination Committee (ICC) of the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Board on the reasonable rate of return on investments and operating and maintenance cost on project proposal of ECOS Sanitary Landfill and Waste Management Corporation as required under Section 2.6.b of the IRR of R.A. 7718.

The Municipal Mayor require the Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile and analyze the difference between their respective records and effect the necessary adjustments; and
Undertake the necessary actions
         for      disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules and procedures provided for in COA Circular No.89-296 dated January 27, 1989.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of dormant accounts; and

Comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said Circular for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission On Audit.
The LDRRMC should identify in its LDRRMFIP only PPAs that are clear and implementable.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive physical count as early as possible to give enough time to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same on or before January 31 of the ensuing year;
Direct the General Services Office to transfer all identified unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) Form for proper disposal and basis to derecognize from the books of accounts; and
Direct all concerned officials to undertake procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the availment of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the coordination between the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in the recording of all Inventory Accounts to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and
The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer maintain the prescribed ledger cards and stock cards, respectively, for each type of Inventory, keep track the actual Supplies Inventory on hand and reconcile the balances of the two inventory records.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Ensure that only necessary expenditures are incurred, observe prudence and fiscal restraint in utilizing government funds which are intended for public purpose and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 to avoid incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures;
Formulate guidelines to set the parameters and regulate the granting of load allowance; and
Require the Accounting Unit to review the monthly billing statement and to reimburse only the amount whichever is less of the actual amount in the monthly billing statement or the ceiling/allowable amount, exclude from payment the following: a) data booster add-ons; b) subscription on sites; c) reconnection fees and other administrative fees; d) roaming texts, data and call charges (unless supported with proof that the same was used for official purposes); and e) monthly amortizations for device.

We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately stop charging the above mentioned expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly limit their charges only to those enumerated under DepEd-DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to include in the certification of absolute necessity explanation/justification as to why claims exceeding the accommodation component are necessary. Claims with justifications found not in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-003 or the Updated Guidelines for the Prevention and Disallowance of Irregular, Unnecessary, Excessive, Extravagant and Unconscionable Expenditures will be disallowed in audit.
We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of hiring vehicles when circumstance do not warrant the same, and that the Municipal Accountant make sure that travel claims for “Van” are duly supported by evidence that such is a customary mode of transport in the area.
Hence, to preclude double payment, it is recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require PNP personnel to attach certifications from the PNP Accounting Office that the said travel claim has not been charged to their office budget.

We recommended and the management agreed to immediately transfer all funds which remain deposited at bank branches outside its locality to the AGDB branch within its jurisdiction so as to avoid any further unnecessary expenses and waste of man hours.

We recommended and the Municipality’s GAD Focal Point Person agreed to:
Maintain its GAD Plan integration in the LGU’s regular activities coupled with compliance with existing GAD laws, policies, and rules; and
Continue addressing gender issues by ensuring gender-responsive Programs, Projects and Activities.

The LCE immediately create an investigating body to conduct a thorough investigation on:
The existence and occurrence of the Returned Checks from the banks for many years; and 
The reasons for none action taken by the Municipal Treasury Office to collect the loss funds of the Municipality;
  
Determine if necessary fines or penalties need to be imposed in addition to the identified sum of Returned Checks; and 

Immediately furnish the Audit Team the result of the investigation, and if deemed necessary, direct the Municipal Legal Officer to file the corresponding legal actions.

The Municipal Accountant to refrain from charging prior year’s expenses that are not booked up as accounts payable to the current year’s appropriation; observe the proper matching of expenses against appropriations; and strictly adhere to pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and P.D. No. 1445.

The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to determine whether all transfers of funds are authorized and received by other funds and to ensure accuracy of its balances.  Also, we recommended that subsidiary ledgers for these accounts be prepared and maintained.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of annual physical inventory of all PPE items, prepare the required RPCPPE and a copy of which be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert extra effort to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

We recommended and the management agreed that:

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile and account for the difference of the Cash-Local Treasury between the cashbooks and the accounting records and thereafter, record the necessary adjustments; and conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to establish the correctness of both records.

2.	The Municipal Treasurer deposit with the authorized depository bank the full amount of her collections daily or not later than the next banking day and discontinue the practice of retaining/withholding part of her collections.

It was recommended and the Management agreed to require:
The Accountant and the General Services Officer to reconcile and analyze the difference between their respective records and effect the necessary adjustments;
The General Services Office to transfer all identified unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) Form as prescribed in Annex 51, Volume 2 of the MNGAS for LGUs, for proper disposal and basis to derecognize from the books of accounts; and
Undertake the necessary actions for disposal in accordance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and the rules and procedures provided for in COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the required analysis of dormant accounts; and
Comply with all the conditions and requirements of the said Circular for the possible request for the write off of the specified dormant receivables from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to determine whether all transfers of funds are authorized and received by other funds and to ensure accuracy of its balances. Also, we recommended that subsidiary ledgers for these accounts be prepared and maintained.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks to visitors and guests on official business and to LGU employees and elected officials in the normal course of conducting official functions and during ordinary/regular staff meetings or conferences; and
Explain why these transactions should not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Stop the practice of charging meal expenses for regular meetings of the LDRRMC against the LGU Funds; and
The Municipal Accountant prepare Adjusting Journal Entries to correct the charging of the expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
Municipal Accountant to refund to the concerned SAs the amount of P202,387.00 balance pertaining to funds of completed projects in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and with the existing Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
If the disposition as to the unspent balances and excess amount of fund transfers is not stipulated in the MOA, be guided by the provisions of Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 relative to the transfer of funds from TF to GF; and
Focal person to review the corresponding MOA, Trust Agreement, or Memorandum of Understanding (MU) governing the utilization of the P2.133 million unutilized funds and coordinate with the SAs whether the same could still be utilized for the furtherance of the objectives of the funds, otherwise facilitate its return to the SAs in compliance with COA Circular 94-013.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The management exert effort in implementing the PPAs identified in its LDRRMFIP in order to increase its capabilities and preparedness to respond in case of disaster or emergencies;
The LDRRMC should identify in its LDRRMFIP only PPAs that are clear and implementable; and
The LDRRMC ensure that only personnel accident insurance for Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers are charged to the LDRRMF.

It was recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
Analyze, trace records, and determine the details of the P64,977.06 unremitted beginning balance and cause its immediate remittance to the BIR and/or make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts, as the case maybe;
Prepare monthly schedule supporting the Due to BIR account to monitor the amounts withheld and remitted and to determine the cause of the noted abnormal balances; and
Strictly comply with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 by ensuring that the amount of all taxes withheld are remitted in full on or before the 10th day of the following month.

It was recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
Analyze, trace records, and determine the details of the unremitted amounts and cause its immediate remittance to the PHIC and/or make the necessary adjustments in the Municipality’s books of accounts, as the case maybe;
Remit   the full amount of contributions withheld from the salaries of the LGU personnel on the month following the month in which withholding was made to avoid any variances, and to hold the person/s responsible for the delayed remittances to personally answer for the penalties and surcharges that may be imposed by the PHIC; and
Recheck/review the accuracy of the total amount of contributions and the amount to be remitted to PHIC every month before making any remittances to avoid over and/or underpayment.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to correctly record the above funds as Trust Liability-DRRM (438);
Include the above funds in the preparation of the CY 2022 LDRRMF Investment   Plan and the status/utilization of the fund on the monthly Report on Utilization of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund; and
Implement    immediately   the    projects identified by the MDRRM Council to be funded from these donations so that the intended beneficiaries will benefit from these funds/projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the management submit the following:

For the payment for the     hauling of Residual Wastes:
Report produced by the system of the weighing bridge at the Malay Sanitary Landfill for each payment.

For the operation and development projects implemented in the Sanitary Landfill:
Statement of Work Accomplished duly verified by the Municipal Engineer;
Inspection Report duly validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative for every payment indicating percentage of completion; and
Certificate of Completion of the project for each payment.
List of Detailed Computations required to support the Program of Works; and

Copy of confirmation/ formal advise from the Investment Coordination Committee (ICC) of the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Board on the reasonable rate of return on investments and operating and maintenance cost on project proposal of ECOS Sanitary Landfill and Waste Management Corporation as required under Section 2.6.b of the IRR of R.A. 7718.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month to the Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommended that the management implement the programmed/targeted projects and activities and close monitoring be made to make sure that these projects are implemented within the time frame.

We recommended the following:

The LCE immediately create an investigating body to:

Conduct a thorough investigation on:
The existence and occurrence of the Returned Checks from the banks for many years; and 
The reasons for none action taken by the Municipal Treasury Office to collect the loss funds of the Municipality;



Determine if necessary fines or penalties need to be imposed in addition to the identified sum of Returned Checks; and 
Immediately furnish the Audit Team the result of the investigation, and if deemed necessary, direct the Municipal Legal Officer to file the corresponding legal actions. 

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review identified erroneous deposits between Municipality’s bank accounts, identify the difference between the additions and deductions amounting to P46,025.04, and cause the immediate transfer of funds to its correct bank account; and

The Municipal Accountant promptly draw JEVs on all identified book reconciling items and henceforth, regularly prepare JEVs for reconciling items that may be identified when preparing their monthly BRS.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the BGCM fund in compliance with GAM for LGUs, Volume III and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;

Create and maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for the BGCM fund in compliance with Section 3.7 of LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020, and Section 143 of GAM for LGUs, Volume I and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and 

Submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents as enumerated in Appendix C6.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer prepare and cause the prompt submission of their monthly Consolidated RAAF including the unsubmitted reports of prior years in the prescribed form together with the individual RAAF of Revenue Collectors to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month. The custodian should see to it that all accountable forms with or without money value are properly accounted for in the report.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant review the JEVs prepared to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.  Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure that all transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of annual physical inventory of all PPE items, prepare the required RPCPPE and a copy of which be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the following year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended the following:
The LCE immediately create an investigating body to:
Conduct a thorough investigation on:
The existence and occurrence of the Returned Checks from the banks for many years; and
The reasons for none action taken by the Municipal Treasury Office to collect the loss funds of the Municipality;
Determine if necessary fines or penalties need to be imposed in addition to the identified sum of Returned Checks; and
Immediately furnish the Audit Team the result of the investigation, and if deemed necessary, direct the Municipal Legal Officer to file the corresponding legal actions.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review identified erroneous deposits between Municipality’s bank accounts, identify the difference between the additions and deductions amounting to P46,025.04, and cause the immediate transfer of funds to its correct bank account; and
The Municipal Accountant promptly draw JEVs on all identified book reconciling items and henceforth, regularly prepare JEVs for reconciling items that may be identified when preparing their monthly BRS.

We recommended and the Office of the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert extra effort to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile and account for the difference of the Cash-Local Treasury between the cashbooks and the accounting records and thereafter, record the necessary adjustments; and conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to establish the correctness of both records.
The Municipal Treasurer deposit with the authorized depository bank the full amount of her collections daily or not later than the next banking day and discontinue the practice of retaining/withholding part of her collections.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Make the necessary adjusting entries to properly account the BGCM fund in compliance with GAM for LGUs, Volume III and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Create and maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for the BGCM fund in compliance with Section 3.7 of LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 and Section 143 of GAM for LGUs, Volume I and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and
Submit to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents as enumerated in Appendix C7.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer prepare and cause the prompt submission of their monthly Consolidated RAAF including the unsubmitted reports of prior years in the prescribed form together with the individual RAAF of Revenue Collectors to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month. The custodian should see to it that all accountable forms with or without money value are properly accounted for in the report.

We recommended that:
	
The designated personnel in the Office of the Accountant should be required to immediately verify and adjust deficiencies between the RPCPPE and the PPELC pursuant to Sec. 13 of the Manual on NGAS, Vol. II;

The GSO shall use/maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment and work animals in their custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and

The Accounting Office must maintain PPELC.

We recommended that the management submit the following:

For the payment for the     hauling of Residual Wastes:
Report produced by the system of the weighing bridge at the Malay Sanitary Landfill for each payment.



For the operation and development projects implemented in the Sanitary Landfill:
Statement of Work Accomplished duly verified by the Municipal Engineer;



Inspection Report duly validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative for every payment indicating percentage of completion; and
Certificate of Completion of the project for each payment.
List of Detailed Computations required to support the Program of Works presented in the Remarks portion of Annex A; and

Copy of confirmation/ formal advise from the Investment Coordination Committee (ICC) of the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Board on the reasonable rate of return on investments and operating and maintenance cost on project proposal of ECOS Sanitary Landfill and Waste Management Corporation as required under Section 2.6.b of the IRR of R.A. 7718.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Management:

Utilize the LDRRMF for its identified projects, programs or activities according to the schedules set in the Annual Investment Program. 

Justify the delayed implementation of the identified projects, programs or activities.
We recommended that the management implement the programmed/targeted projects and activities and close monitoring be made to make sure that these projects are implemented within the time frame.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive directs all the Department Heads concerned to prepare the AAPSI related to CY 2018 AAR findings and recommendations and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as required by Section 93 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2018; and

The management shall submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations within 60 days from receipt of the succeeding COA Annual Audit Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that:

1. The inventory taking shall be done by the office and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE on the results thereof; and
The RPCPPE shall then be reconciled with accounting records in order to ensure that PPE account balances in the financial statements are fairly presented.

We recommended that the Management:

Utilize the LDRRMF for its identified projects, programs or activities according to the schedules set in Annual Investment Program; and


Justify the delayed implementation of the identified projects, programs or activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 
Prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the

prescribed period.

Prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the CY 2018 on all depository accounts, duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the treasury office.
Section 7.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 should be put in force as the evidence may warrant.

We recommended that:

The Local Chief Executive directs all the Department Heads concerned to prepare  the AAPSI related to CY 2017 AAR findings and recommendations and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as required by Section 88 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2017; and
The management shall within 60 days from receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that:
The designated personnel in the Office of the Accountant should be required to immediately verify and adjust deficiencies between the RPCPPE and the PPELC pursuant to Sec. 13 of the Manual on NGAS, Vol. II;
The GSO shall use/maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment and work animals in their custody to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and
The Accounting Office must maintain PPELC.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant review the JEVs prepared to identify the causes of the discrepancies and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.  Likewise, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made, to ensure that all transactions are properly and accurately recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the management submit the following:
For the payment for the hauling of Residual Wastes:
Report produced by the system of the weighing bridge at the Malay Sanitary Landfill for each payment.
For the operation and development projects implemented in the Sanitary Landfill:
Statement of Work Accomplished duly verified by the Municipal Engineer;
Inspection Report duly validated by the Municipal Engineer or his authorized representative for every payment indicating percentage of completion; and
Certificate of Completion of the project for each payment.
List of Detailed Computations required to support the Program of Works presented in the Remarks portion of Annex A; and
Copy of confirmation/formal advise from the Investment Coordination Committee (ICC) of the National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Board on the reasonable rate of return on investments and operating and maintenance cost on project proposal of ECOS Sanitary Landfill and Waste Management Corporation as required under Section 2.6.b of the IRR of R.A. 7718.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under Sections 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Management:
Utilize the LDRRMF for its identified projects, programs or activities according to the schedules set in the Annual Investment Program.
Justify the delayed implementation of the identified projects, programs or activities.
We recommended that the management implement the programmed/targeted projects and activities and close monitoring be made to make sure that these projects are implemented within the time frame.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive directs all the Department Heads concerned to prepare the AAPSI related to CY 2017 AAR findings and recommendations and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as required by Section 88 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2017; and
The management shall submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on the audit findings and recommendations within 60 days from receipt of the succeeding COA Annual Audit Report.

We recommended that:

The management properly evaluate the mode of implementation in performing programs, projects or activities in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
Explain why the transaction was implemented under the Public-Private Partnership which should have been under the R.A. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Act.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the Municipal Officials concerned to submit copy of the contracts together with the supporting documents within five (5) working days upon perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that management plan properly the identification of priority projects to be funded under the 20% DF to guarantee its completion within the target set and to provide immediate benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management: 

1.  Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections with the   AGDB, daily or not later than the next banking day;  

2.   Follow strictly the rules and regulations stated in Paragraphs 1 and 4, Section 69 of P.D. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I; and

3.  Submit justification why:

The amount of P9.757 million was not deposited intact on January 3, 2019, and

The remaining balance of P204,179.26 which still not deposited as of February 12, 2019.

1.Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all  collections with the   AGDB, daily or not later than the next banking day;  

2. Follow strictly the rules and regulations stated in Paragraphs 1 and 4, Section 69 of P.D. 1445 and Section 32 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I; and

3.Submit justification why the amount of P6.588 million was not deposited to the authorized depository bank intact on January 3, 2018, the first banking day of CY 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 
Prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; and 

Prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for the CY 2017 on all depository accounts, duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the treasury office.

Then all submitted reports and supporting documents by the accountable officers shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts of which the same shall be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under par. 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that:

1. The inventory taking shall be done by the office and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE on the results thereof; and
2. The RPCPPE shall then be reconciled with accounting records in order to ensure that PPE account balances in the financial statements are fairly presented.

Prepare the bank reconciliation statement every month as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendations:
The Local Chief Executive directs all the Department Heads concerned to prepare  the AAPSI related to CY 2017 AAR findings and recommendations and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor as required by Section 88 of the General Provisions of General Appropriations Act (GAA) of 2017; and
The management shall within 60 days from receipt of the COA Annual Audit Report submit to the COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the year-end 2016-2017 Financial Statements and all supporting schedules and reports with the accompanying Statement of Management Responsibility for Financial Statements to the COA Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer foot, rule, balance and certify correct immediately at the end of the month below the last entry in his Cashbook/CRR/CDR/ CkDR or its equivalent in accordance with the above regulations.

Recommendations:

Develop a comprehensive juvenile intervention program covering at least 3-year period;

Identify Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) that responds to the special needs, problems, interest and concerns of children. Follow DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 in identifying PPAs; and

Implement the approved appropriation for the identified PPAs of the LCPC.

We recommended that the Management:

Be realistic and economical in planning the work to be done  in the projects to be implemented to avoid the occurrence of the above mentioned deficiencies; and
Refund the amount of P43,340.74 and submit to the office of the Audit Team the corresponding Official Receipt.

We recommended that the Management:
Be abreast of the current prevailing price of materials to be procured in   order that costing or estimates in the POW should be realistic;  
Follow strictly and submit the documentary requirements as prescribed under COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and
Justify why there was an over estimated cost of P550.00 per cu. m. of     backfilling materials as against the actual bid price.

We recommended that proper planning and efficient implementation should be made on identified Projects, Programs and Activities under the Solid Waste management Services to guarantee its completion within the target set and to ensure efficient and effective delivery of services to the constituents.

We recommended that the Management:

Provide and post within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices in the project site.

Follow strictly the provisions of COA Cir. No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

Justify why signboards/billboards were not provided in the POW.

We recommended that the new Municipal Accountant must prepare and submit the year-end Financial Statements and supporting schedules and reports with the accompanying Statement of Management Responsibility for Financial Statements to the COA Auditor or Audit Team Leader within five (5) calendar days from receipt hereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enforce daily the submission of all the reports and documents from the Municipal Treasurer. All submitted reports and supporting documents shall immediately be recorded in the books of accounts. The same shall then be submitted within ten (10) days of the ensuing month  to the  Office of the Audit Team Leader as mandated under par. 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that Honorable Mayor:

Initiate the payment of employees’ salaries, allowances, fringe benefits and other emoluments through the bank, as a means of improving fiscal management in accordance with Section 4(4) and (8) of PD 1445, with the expected result of minimizing the attendant risks identified with cash-paid payrolls and easing the effects of manpower attrition;

Make a representation/ arrange with any government bank like the LBP for an opening of individual ATM savings accounts by the employees through a Memorandum of Agreement; and

Adopt the payroll-thru-bank scheme upon approval of the Memorandum of Agreement to strengthen fiscal management.
Explain why the bidder was declared eligible despite the deficiencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Verify and examine carefully the supporting documents as to its completeness as prescribed under the above mentioned COA Circular for common government transactions before signing the disbursement vouchers to avoid delay in the post audit; and

Submit the documents enumerated above to the office of the audit team as soon as possible.

We recommended that the Management:

Stop the practice of transferring the cash advances to the project engineers in charge of the project for disbursement and strictly follow the rules and regulations stipulated in the above mentioned COA Circular; and

Designate a permanent employee to act as special disbursing officer in charge in disbursing payrolls in the field and the same shall be bonded in accordance with law.

We recommended the following:

Management should require the budget committee during the preparation of annual budget for 20% DF to include only P/P/A/s which are feasible, and could be completed as to target date of execution, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership. Likewise, there should be regular monitoring for timely and efficient implementation of these projects; and

Management should consider the impact of the project in prioritizing its implementation.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and Accounting Office should prioritize preparation and submission of the reports on LDRRMF to aid stakeholders/management in performing their decision making functions. The reports should contain complete and relevant information.

We recommended that the management justify/explain the abovementioned observations noted. All the documents attached to the vouchers be properly filled-up as to date, number, etc. to avoid the payment of liquidated damages. The same should be in accordance with the prescribed format. Also, please submit the lacking documents.

We recommended the following to the Management:

Require the budget committee during the preparation of annual budget to include only P/P/A/s which are feasible, related and could be completed as to target date of execution, after taking into consideration the priorities of the LGU leadership. Likewise, there should be regular monitoring for timely and efficient implementation of the projects; and

Consider the impact of the project in prioritizing its implementation.

We recommended the following to the Management:

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE; and

Submit to this office the Report on Physical Count of  Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Management:

Properly monitor the expiration of the bond of the accountable officers and apply immediately for the renewal;

Increase the maximum accountability of the disbursing officer in accordance with law; and

Justify why the disbursing officer was allowed to cash advance beyond the maximum accountability.

We recommended that the Management:

Plan the program and projects in an economical and proximate manner; 

Uninstalled construction materials due to excess in planning should be properly accounted for and safeguarded for used in other similar projects; and

Justify/explain why the following deficiencies will not be disallowed in audit:

Uninstalled construction materials in the amount of P1,230,367.00;

Excess payment of labor in the amount of P 123,575.00 against POW; and

Difference of P444,352.44 and  P830,550.00 against COA Evaluation on labor and materials, respectively.
We recommended that management should require the Accountant to submit the monthly accounts to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.

We recommended that the Local Government should conduct physical inventory of supplies semestrally and Property, Plant and Equipment annually and submit the required report thereon pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.
